svg.title: TradersApp — System Design
svg.desc: Full TradersApp architecture: Frontend, BFF, analysis-service, ML Engine, Redis, Feast, Kafka, ML ops, Airflow, MLflow, MinIO, PostgreSQL, Prometheus, Grafana, Loki, Jaeger, Trivy, Keycloak, Gitea, Woodpecker CI, Helm, k3s, Infisical, External Secrets, OPA.
svg.text: TradersApp · every component · full width · clean layout
Browser → Frontend → BFF → gRPC seam → analysis-service → ML Engine. Target: 50ms SLA.
Frontend: React + Vite · BFF: Node.js Express · ML Engine: Python FastAPI
Market feeds + operator labels → Airflow + Great Expectations → validated data → Kafka → MLflow registry → retrain
DQ gates block dirty data · Closed-loop feedback via Kafka
DAGs · DQ gates · Blocks dirty data
Prometheus scrapes · Grafana visualizes · Loki aggregates logs · Jaeger traces requests
Catches: SLA breach · drift · retrain failure · infra regression
Container vulnerability scanning · CI integration · k8s admission
Source → CI pipeline → build → Helm chart → k3s deploy → secrets injected from Infisical
Gitea + Woodpecker · Helm on k3s · Infisical · OPA · External Secrets Operator
Inside ml-engine/: models (HMM regime, FP-FK, LightGBM, Mamba, PSO, Alpha) · features · training · inference · optimization
No global state · Singleton models · Guardrails on every output
Current reality — what is actually extracted
analysis-service is the ONLY extracted bounded context on the live request path.
Ingestion + learning still live inside ML Engine. Platform ops (MLflow, observability, CI/CD, secrets) are self-hosted and fully operational.
HTTP: Frontend↔BFF · gRPC: BFF↔analysis-service↔ML Engine · Redis/Kafka/Feast: data plane
One row per top-level folder in the monorepo. Bold = deployed runtime. Italic = configuration or data.
→ React frontend · Vite bundler · Zustand state · port 5173
→ Node.js BFF · Express · Auth · Rate limit · Circuit breaker
→ Python FastAPI · ML models · Training · Inference · port 8001
→ Telegram bot · Web server · User registry
→ Helm charts · k3s manifests · Secrets · OPA policies
